name: UNSC or Insurrectionist Army Soldier
description: Earth or beyond, we'll fight for the soil under our boots.

skillChoices.label: Skills of choice
skillChoices.notes: The character begins with 2 skills of their choosing at +10 at character creation.

notes: Insurrectionist option: if chosen, any item without the [I] tag costs 25% more. Insurrectionists gain all weapon and vehicle modifications at half cR price.

factionChoice.prompt: Choose this soldier's allegiance. UNSC is the default.

name: Colonial Militiaman
description: We won't be getting reinforcements for another week. Too bad for them, that means this'll only be a fair fight for a short time.

skillChoices.label: Skills of choice
skillChoices.notes: The Militiaman begins with two skills of their choosing at +10 at character creation.

notes: Physical attributes: 130 centimeters (4'3 ft.) - 200 centimeters (6'6 ft.), 45 kilograms (100 lb.) - 117 kilograms (260 lb.). Insurrectionist option: if chosen, any item without the [I] tag costs 25% more. Insurrectionists gain all weapon and vehicle modifications at half cR price.

factionChoice.prompt: Choose this militiaman's allegiance. UNSC is the default.

name: UNSC or Insurrectionist Marine Soldier
description: We're best of the best, and we'll prove it to anyone who disagrees.

skillChoices.label: Skills of choice
skillChoices.notes: The Marine begins with four skills of their choosing at Trained at character creation.

equipmentPackCatalog.notes: External mission-loadout equipment packs linked for Marine Soldier.

notes: Physical attributes: 130 centimeters (4'3 ft.) - 200 centimeters (6'6 ft.), 45 kilograms (100 lb.) - 117 kilograms (260 lb.). Insurrectionist option: if chosen, any item without the [I] tag costs 25% more. Insurrectionists gain all weapon and vehicle modifications at half cR price.

factionChoice.prompt: Choose this marine's allegiance. UNSC is the default.

name: UNSC or Insurrectionist Air Force Soldier
description: Just remember, boys. We're the apex predators.

skillChoices.label: Skills of choice
skillChoices.notes: The Air Force Soldier begins with three skills of their choosing at Trained at character creation.

notes: Vehicle: The Air Force Soldier may purchase any UNSC Military Vehicle at one third its listed cR price; this vehicle is added to starting equipment and cannot be sold or traded. Between missions, the character may swap to another vehicle by paying one third of the new vehicle cost. The vehicle is considered the party's vehicle, not solely the Air Force pilot's. Physical attributes: 130 centimeters (4'3 ft.) - 200 centimeters (6'6 ft.), 45 kilograms (100 lb.) - 117 kilograms (260 lb.). Insurrectionist option: if chosen, any item without the [I] tag costs 25% more. Insurrectionists gain all weapon and vehicle modifications at half cR price.

factionChoice.prompt: Choose this air force soldier's allegiance. UNSC is the default.

name: UNSC or Insurrectionist Navy Technician
description: I can do it blindfolded, sir.

skillChoices.label: Skills of choice
skillChoices.notes: The Navy Technician begins with three Skills of their choosing at Trained at Character Creation.

educationChoices.label: Educations of choice
educationChoices.notes: The Navy Technician begins with 3 +5 Educations at Character Creation.

notes: Physical attributes: 130 centimeters (4'3 ft.) - 200 centimeters (6'6 ft.), 45 kilograms (100 lb.) - 117 kilograms (260 lb.). Insurrectionist option: if chosen, any item without the [I] tag costs 25% more. Insurrectionists gain all weapon and vehicle modifications at half cR price.

factionChoice.prompt: Choose this navy technician's allegiance. UNSC is the default.

name: UNSC MARINE CORPS ORBITAL DROP SHOCK TROOPERS (ODST)
description: Feet first into hell.

skillChoices.label: Skills of choice
skillChoices.notes: The ODST begins with three skills of their choosing at +10 at character creation.

branchTransition.notes: Scaffold only: Becoming an ODST is deferred to Advancement flow; do not enforce during character creation.

notes: Character creation import intentionally ignores the 'Becoming an ODST' rules section for now. This will be handled later via Advancement flow. Physical attributes: 130 centimeters (4'3 ft.) - 200 centimeters (6'6 ft.), 45 kilograms (100 lb.) - 117 kilograms (260 lb.).

name: UNSC/ONI SECTION I OPERATIVE
description: <\ CLEARANCE: Classified [Level [redacted] and above]

oniSectionOne.gmApprovalText: This Soldier Type should only be taken with GM Approval. The GM is advised to treat it with caution, as revealing a Spy in the players ranks can lead to distrust and Dissension within the ranks.

rankScaffold.notes: Scaffold only: rank mechanics are not automated yet.

supportScaffold.notes: Scaffold only: apply +1 extra Support Point at character creation and mission rewards.

unscSupportCostScaffold.notes: Scaffold only: ONI Section I discounted UNSC support costs.

notes: Approval is handled as a GM-approval prompt, not an applied trait item.

factionChoice.prompt: Choose this Section I Operative's affiliation. UNSC is default; ONI is available.

name: UNSC ORION PROJECT AUGMENTED SOLDIER (SPARTAN 1/1.1)
description: You were the best of the best, and we made you better.

skillChoices.label: Skills of choice
skillChoices.notes: The ORION Soldier begins with six skills of their choosing at Trained at character creation.

orionAugmentation.notes: Scaffold only: Becoming an ORION Soldier is deferred to Advancement flow; do not enforce during character creation. Automation for Resistant and ORION augmentation effects will be implemented later.

notes: Character creation import intentionally ignores the 'Becoming an ORION Soldier' rules section for now. This will be handled later via Advancement flow.

name: UNSC/ONI SPARTAN II AUGMENTED SOLDIER
description: You have been called upon to serve, you will be trained... and you will become the best we can make of you. You will be the protectors of Earth and all her colonies.

skillChoices.label: Skills of choice
skillChoices.notes: The Spartan gains four Skills at +10 at Character Creation.

requiredUpbringing.notes: Spartan IIs are required to take the Military Upbringing. Any existing non-Military upbringing is removed when this soldier type is applied.

notes: Weapons Expert: Full training in all weapon types and factions granted automatically. Broken: Emotionally Broken ability granted automatically; Fast Foot is not automated at this time. Augmented: Carbide Ceramic Ossification, Occipital Capillary Reversal, and Superconducting Fibrication of Neural Dendrites traits granted automatically. Mjolnir armor selection is prompted during character creation and filtered by campaign year.

name: UNSC/ONI SPARTAN III AUGMENTED SOLDIER
description: The UNSC's cost-effective Spartan initiative. Spartan IIIs are numerous, mission-focused supersoldiers with near-equivalent augmentation performance.

skillChoices.label: Skills of choice
skillChoices.notes: The Spartan gains four Skills at +10 at Character Creation.

allowedUpbringings.notes: Spartan IIIs can use Military or War-Orphan upbringing only. Any other upbringing is removed when this soldier type is applied.

gammaCompanyOption.prompt: Enable optional Spartan III Gamma Company rules?

notes: Weapons Expert: Full weapon/faction training granted automatically. Fast Foot granted at character creation. Spartan II augmentation traits and Squad Up (Spartan) are granted automatically. Mjolnir armor selection is prompted during character creation and filtered by campaign year. Optional Gamma Company grants Adrenaline Rush and enables Smoother Drug usage tracking in Medical.

name: UNSC SPARTAN IV AUGMENTED SOLDIER
description: You are Spartans now. You stand side by side with your Spartan brothers and sisters.

skillChoices.label: Skills of choice
skillChoices.notes: The Spartan gains four Skills at +10 at Character Creation.

ordinanceReady.notes: Scaffold only for now; no automatic combat mechanic is applied yet.

notes: Weapons Expert: Full weapon/faction training granted automatically. Fast Foot granted at character creation. Spartan augmentation traits are granted automatically. Ordinance Ready is granted as a non-mechanical scaffold trait. Mjolnir armor selection is prompted during character creation and filtered by campaign year.

name: UNSC SMART AI
description: You're not just a soldier — you're a thinking weapon. Every battlefield calculation runs through you in microseconds.

skillChoices.label: Skills of choice
skillChoices.notes: The Smart AI begins with 4 skills of their choosing at +10 at character creation.

smartAi.notes: Smart AI bodyless restrictions and host/station requirements apply. No Specialization Pack is enforced — specialization selection is disabled for this soldier type.

notes: Smart AIs are non-corporeal intelligences operating through hardware interfaces and host platforms. They cannot select a Specialization Pack. Interface abilities refresh each encounter and are tracked with uses and cooldown.

name: Human Civilian
description: I'm not sitting by and watching my world, and everything in it, go to hell.
notes: Civilians have two choices for their Specialization Pack: spend 1,800 XP to build their own Specialization Pack of their choice, or wait and join a Military branch and gain a Specialization Pack worth 2,100 XP. If the Civilian built their own Specialization Pack, they cannot take a Military branch Specialization Pack later.

name: United Earth Government Police Force
description: When the going gets tough, we'll get tougher.

skillChoices.label: Skill of choice
skillChoices.notes: The Policeman begins with one Skill of their choosing at +10 at Character Creation.

educationGrants.name: Human Law
educationGrants.notes: The Policeman begins with the Human Law +10 Education at Character Creation.

notes: Police Officers serve the United Earth Government, enforcing law and order across human-controlled space. They are limited in their access to military hardware — UNSC weaponry costs double unless otherwise specified, and Covenant and Forerunner armories are entirely off-limits.

name: Covenant Unggoy
description: We're gonna go down in history!

trainingPathChoice.prompt: Choose Unggoy training path. Combat Trained is the default for most Covenant campaigns.

advancementOptions.label: Becoming a Deacon
advancementOptions.summary: Promotes this Unggoy into Covenant religious command as a Deacon.
advancementOptions.requirements: Spend a minimum of 500 XP on Educations.
advancementOptions.details: Deacons no longer pay double XP for Educations, gain Leadership perks, and may purchase Goblin or Gorgon vehicles at half price.
advancementOptions.notes: Scaffold only for now. Selection is recorded in Advancements > Soldier Type Advancements.

notes: Unggoy are modeled as combat-trained by default because most Covenant campaigns use that path. Civilian path remains available as an opt-in at apply time.

name: Covenant Sangheili
description: Your bloodline carries duty, and your duty carries steel.

trainingPathChoice.prompt: Choose Sangheili training path. Combat Trained is the default for most Covenant campaigns.

advancementOptions.label: Bloodline
advancementOptions.summary: Sangheili bloodline options that reshape role and social standing.
advancementOptions.details: Choose a bloodline track to define clan obligations and inherited expectations.
advancementOptions.notes: Scaffold only. Mechanical branch effects will be added later.

advancementOptions.label: Ancestral Honor
advancementOptions.summary: Formalize honor-bound obligations and legacy penalties/rewards.
advancementOptions.details: Track honor debts, oaths, and social consequences as part of Sangheili advancement.
advancementOptions.notes: Scaffold only. Mechanical enforcement will be added later.

notes: Sangheili include civilian baseline profile with optional Combat Training package and soldier-type advancement scaffolding.

name: Covenant Jiralhanae
description: Your pack, your rage, and your prey define you.

trainingPathChoice.prompt: Choose Jiralhanae training path. Combat Trained is the default for most Covenant campaigns.

advancementOptions.label: Pack Leader
advancementOptions.summary: Advance into Jiralhanae pack leadership and become Chieftain.
advancementOptions.requirements: Leadership 50 or narrative succession within the pack. Not available to civilians without combat training.
advancementOptions.details: The Jiralhanae becomes Pack Leader and Chieftain, trading their starting weapons from their equipment pack for a Gravity Hammer and gaining supreme authority within the pack.
advancementOptions.notes: A Jiralhanae may also seize the position from another within the same pack; otherwise it is an act of war between packs.

notes: Berserker grants +20 to smell-based Perception Tests. Journey of the Hunter follows sequential mythic characteristic advancement pricing. Civilian Jiralhanae may spend 1,800 XP to build their own specialization pack or wait to become combat trained and gain one.

name: Covenant Kig-Yar Ruutian
description: Snipers, pirates, and mercenaries. Where do I sign up?

trainingPathChoice.prompt: Choose Kig-Yar Ruutian training path. Combat Trained is the default for most Covenant campaigns.

notes: Leaping is not yet automated for Kig-Yar variants; Ruutians double their relevant Characteristic Modifier when figuring Jump and Leap Actions. Civilian Kig-Yar may spend 1,800 XP to build a specialization pack or wait to become Combat Trained and gain one.

name: Covenant Kig-Yar T'vaoan
description: Saw one of those chickens nearly catch up to my Mongoose.

trainingPathChoice.prompt: Choose Kig-Yar T'vaoan training path. Combat Trained is the default for most Covenant campaigns.

notes: Leaping is not yet automated for Kig-Yar variants; T'vaoan triple their relevant Characteristic Modifier when figuring Jump and Leap Actions.

name: Covenant Kig-Yar Ibie'Shan
description: Betcha he'll stop attacking you for some ham.

trainingPathChoice.prompt: Choose Kig-Yar Ibie'Shan training path. Combat Trained is the default for most Covenant campaigns.

notes: Leaping is not yet automated for Kig-Yar variants; Ibie'Shan double their relevant Characteristic Modifier when figuring Jump and Leap Actions.

name: Covenant Yanme'e
description: They're like giant cockroaches, except angrier.

trainingPathChoice.prompt: Choose Yanme'e training path. Civilian matches the source page baseline; Combat Training adds the separate 1,600 Experience package.

skillChoices.label: Combat Training Skills
skillChoices.notes: The Combat Trained Yanme'e gains two Skills of their choosing at +10.

naturalArmorScaffold.notes: Scaffold only. Natural Armor 10 is recorded here for future automation and is not yet applied automatically to DR calculations.

notes: Educations: The Yanme'e doubles their Intellect Modifier when figuring how many Educations they may learn. Civilians have two choices: spend 1,800 Experience to build their own Specialization Pack of their choice, or wait to become Combat Trained and gain a Specialization Pack. Natural Armor 10 is scaffolded in rule flags for now and is not yet automated.

name: Covenant San'Shyuum
description: Will they not see us as Gods? Will our power not push us towards our Great Journey?

trainingPathChoice.prompt: Choose San'Shyuum training path. Combat Trained is the default for most Covenant campaigns.

advancementOptions.label: Becoming a Cleric
advancementOptions.summary: Once a San'Shyuum has had Combat Training, they may spend 800 XP to become a Ministry Cleric.
advancementOptions.requirements: Requires Combat Training. Clerics receive an extra 0.5g to figuring their pay.
advancementOptions.details: Medical Role: The Cleric role for a San'Shyuum is that of a Medic. Clerics gain a second Equipment Kit of Medical tools and medication. Medication: The Cleric also gains the Medication Skill at +20.
advancementOptions.notes: Scaffold only. Second Equipment Kit and Medication skill +20 are not yet automated. Equipment: 5 Magnetic Splints, 10 sets of Sealant Mesh, 6 Thermal Blankets, Syringe Set, 30 cR worth of UNSC Medical Equipment (used as a Covenant variant).

advancementOptions.label: Becoming a Prophet
advancementOptions.summary: Once a San'Shyuum has become a Cleric, they may spend 1,000 XP to advance into the Prophet ministry.
advancementOptions.details: Truth: Once a San'Shyuum has become a Prophet, they are able to purchase from the Forerunner Armory at 2 times the cost listed. New Pack: The San'Shyuum is able to replace their Equipment Kit with a Prophetic Equipment Pack.
advancementOptions.notes: Scaffold only. Forerunner Armory pricing and Prophetic Equipment Pack replacement are not yet automated.

notes: Gravity effects are not fully automated; San'Shyuum are adapted to 0.5x Earth gravity and come with a Gravity Belt that offsets higher-gravity penalties while active. Civilians may spend 1,800 XP to build their own specialization pack or wait to become Combat Trained and gain one.

name: Covenant Huragok
description: Ancient Forerunner engineers with unmatched technical mastery and limited direct combat focus.

factionChoice.prompt: Choose Huragok faction before selecting a variant.

infusionOption.prompt: Banished Huragok may take Infusion (costs 1000 XP). Apply Infusion now?

advancementScaffold.label: Infusion
advancementScaffold.summary: Banished-only Huragok advancement that replaces Overshield projection with an offensive radius.
advancementScaffold.details: Infused Huragok can attack without Pacifist Courage tests, gain +10 TOU and -10 INT, and receive the Infusion Radius weapon scaffold.
advancementScaffold.notes: Scaffolded for future multi-race advancement chains (Deacon, Cleric, Pack Leader, etc.).

notes: Apply flow: choose faction, choose variant (Engineer or Lifeworker), then Banished characters may optionally apply Infusion.

name: Covenant San'Shyuum Prelate
description: There were not many San'Shyuum who could match your skills or your devotion - and now perhaps there are none.

allowedUpbringings.notes: Prelates can only use Nobility, Aristocracy, or Fugitive upbringing.

advancementOptions.label: Becoming a Cleric
advancementOptions.summary: The Prelate may spend 800 XP to take on the Cleric role (via the Prophetic trait).
advancementOptions.requirements: Clerics receive an extra 0.5g to figuring their pay.
advancementOptions.details: Medical Role: The Cleric role for a San'Shyuum is that of a Medic. Clerics gain a second Equipment Kit of Medical tools and medication. Medication: The Cleric also gains the Medication Skill at +20.
advancementOptions.notes: Scaffold only. Second Equipment Kit and Medication skill +20 are not yet automated.

advancementOptions.label: Becoming a Prophet
advancementOptions.summary: Once a Prelate has taken the Cleric advancement, they may spend 1,000 XP to advance into the Prophet ministry.
advancementOptions.details: Truth: The Prelate is able to purchase from the Forerunner Armory at 2 times the cost listed. New Pack: The San'Shyuum is able to replace their Equipment Kit with a Prophetic Equipment Pack.
advancementOptions.notes: Scaffold only. Forerunner Armory pricing and Prophetic Equipment Pack replacement are not yet automated.

notes: Support Points: High Support grants 5 Support Points at character creation — scaffold only, not yet auto-applied to the support field. Gravity: San'Shyuum Prelates are genetically modified and do not receive the -10 AGI gravity penalty at >=1.0g. World gravity still affects normal derived values (movement, carry capacity, lift/push limits).

name: Covenant Mgalekgolo
description: Hunter pair. If one of them is silent, that is when you panic.

allowedUpbringings.notes: Mgalekgolo are locked to Military, Laborer, Fugitive, or Wastelander upbringings.

phenomeChoice.prompt: Select the Lekgolo Phenome Culture (Ref p.77).

choices.label: Dipholekgolo
choices.notes: Enhanced perceptive tech sensing per page 77.

notes: Hunter bonds, colony dependence, and bonded-pair assumptions are represented in notes and traits rather than hard automation. Carry Weight is fixed at 5,000 kg and is unaffected by armor.

name: Covenant Mgalekgolo Slugmen
description: Hunters and Jackals had a baby. Keep your heads low.

allowedUpbringings.notes: Slugmen are locked to Military, Laborer, Fugitive, or Wastelander upbringings.

choices.label: Dipholekgolo
choices.notes: Enhanced perceptive technology base. Can use Perceptive Range and tech sensing in 1/2 range.

choices.label: Sbaolekgolo
choices.notes: Larger-bodied phenome. Size Category is one category larger (Giant, or Immense with Imposing).

notes: Infantry-variant Lekgolo. Carry Weight is fixed at 1,500 kg and is unaffected by armor. Bond Brother and phenome selection follow standard rules.

name: Fringe Covenant Yonhet
description: Gotta give her credit for her shout-out to the Yonhet, though. I mean, nobody remembers those guys.

trainingPathChoice.prompt: Choose Yonhet training path. Combat Training is limited to GM Approval. Yonhet are almost always traders, smugglers, and politicians.

name: Banished Mgalekgolo Goliath
description: It’s like a walking tank that bulldozes everything.

allowedUpbringings.notes: Mgalekgolo are locked to Military, Laborer, Fugitive, or Wastelander upbringings.

phenomeChoice.prompt: Select the Lekgolo Phenome Culture (Ref p.77).

choices.label: Dipholekgolo
choices.notes: Enhanced perceptive tech sensing per page 77.

notes: Hunter bonds, colony dependence, and bonded-pair assumptions are represented in notes and traits rather than hard automation. Carry Weight is fixed at 5,000 kg and is unaffected by armor.

name: Banished and Endless Gasgira
description: They’re the smelliest bastards I have ever seen.

trainingPathChoice.prompt: Choose Gasgira training path. Combat Trained is the default for Banished campaigns.

flightScaffold.notes: Scaffold only. Gasgira flight support is noted for future automation and is not yet applied automatically.

notes: Banished Gasgira use the civilian Gasgira baseline from the source card. Upbringing Any is treated as unrestricted and is intentionally not written into the header. Combat Training is the default path. Limited applies only to civilians. Armor and flight remain scaffolded only for future automation.

name: Promethean Soldier
description: Sentient, intelligent, and resourceful. A major upgrade from those braindead sentinels we managed to trick with a rock and a piece of string.

name: Promethean Knight
description: Sentient, intelligent, and resourceful. A major upgrade from those braindead sentinels we managed to trick with a rock and a piece of string.

dualWieldScaffold.notes: Scaffold only. Promethean Knights ignore dual-wield penalties and cannot drop dual-wielded weapons once dual-wield automation exists.

name: Promethean Cavalier
description: Life is a fleeting vessel too fragile and small to hold a true mind
